sensitivity: 0.984 (worst-case with inconclusive as negative); 0.990 (best-case with inconclusive as positive) for any suspicious findings
specificity: 0.970 (best-case with inconclusive as negative); 0.958 (worst-case with inconclusive as positive) for any suspicious findings
auc: 0.974 (aided reading); 0.825 (unaided reading) for detection of any suspicious radiographic finding in reader study
intended_use: Fetal EchoScan is a machine learning-based computer-assisted diagnosis (CADx) software device indicated as an adjunct to fetal heart ultrasound examination in pregnant women aged 18 or older undergoin
intended_use_full: Fetal EchoScan is a machine learning-based computer-assisted diagnosis (CADx) software device indicated as an adjunct to fetal heart ultrasound examination in pregnant women aged 18 or older undergoing second-trimester anatomic ultrasound exams. When utilized by an interpreting physician, Fetal EchoScan provides information regarding the presence of any of the following suspicious radiographic findings: overriding artery, septal defect at the cardiac crux, abnormal relationship of the outflow tracts, enlarged cardiothoracic ratio, right ventricular to left ventricular size discrepancy, tricuspid valve to mitral valve annular size discrepancy, pulmonary valve to aortic valve annular size discrepancy, cardiac axis deviation.
target_condition: Fetal congenital heart defects (suspicious morphological findings)
ground_truth: Three pediatric cardiologists independently assessed the presence or absence of each of the eight findings; majority voting was used to establish reference standard
other_metrics: Reader study mean sensitivity for aided condition: 0.935 vs unaided 0.782 (+15.3% increase); Reader study mean specificity for aided condition: 0.970 vs unaided 0.759 (+21.1% increase); Device had conclusive output for 99.0% (95% CI 98.1-99.5) of exams; Stratified analysis showed consistent performance across gestational age, maternal age, BMI, race, ultrasound machine make/model, image quality, reader specialty, and reader country.
